items.description: It’s no coincidence that extreme heat is engulfing huge swaths of Asia, Europe and North America all at the same time.

items.title: Expectant mothers in Cuba struggle under fuel blockade
items.description: The BBC's Will Grant speaks to two women experiencing starkly different realities as the country's energy crisis impacts their pregnancies.

items.title: Oil price steadies for now but cost of petrol soars around the world
items.description: The price of a barrel of oil has steadied with signs the US is open to ending the war against Iran, even if the Strait of Hormuz remains closed. But at the petrol pump, consumers are still paying the price for the global supply chain disruption.

items.title: A Gaza mother waits in agony: Did Israel kill her daughter, or arrest her?
items.description: Tahrir Abu Mady has her daughter Malak's death certificate. But a prisoner's list suggests she might have been arrested.

items.title: Stories that caught our eye: March 21 to 27
items.description: A selection of the stories that caught our eye over the last seven days in Argentina. Leer más

items.title: Football club loses compensation case over Argentina striker Emiliano Sala's death
items.description: French court dismisses Cardiff City's compensation claim – valued at 122 million euros (US$140 million – in a financial dispute with Ligue 1 club Nantes over the 2019 death of Argentine striker Emiliano Sala. Leer más

items.title: In Sudan, sexual violence is a "weapon" among many
items.description: Sudan's paramilitary Rapid Support Forces and allied militias are using sexual violence as a "weapon of war" in Darfur to control civilians, medical charity Doctors Without Borders said Tuesday.

items.title: Arcor, Danone take control of La Serenísima, reshaping Argentina dairy market
items.description: National dairy producer La Serenísima has new owners, Grupo Arcor and Danone will share equal control of Mastellone Hermanos SA, owner of La Serenísima brand; Value of deal not disclosed. Leer más

items.title: News24 | US intelligence chief Gabbard ‘softer’ on nuclear threat, but Trump eyes Iran oil
items.description: US President Donald Trump hinted at differences among his top aides on their approach to Iran, saying that his intelligence chief, Tulsi Gabbard, was “softer” than him on curbing Tehran’s nuclear ambitions.

items.title: Long queues in Myanmar as Iran war fuel crisis deepens
items.description: Drivers are queuing for hours at petrol stations in Myanmar as the Iran war continues to send shockwaves across the globe.

items.title: Fuel rations and free buses: How countries are responding to rising oil prices
items.description: Governments around the world have introduced measures to limit the impact of price increases.

items.title: Iran-Israel war highlights: Trump again warns Iran to open Strait of Hormuz
items.description: The U.S. President threatens to obliterate Iran’s Kharg island if no deal reached ‘shortly’; Israeli media reports Haifa oil refinery hit after attack

items.title: Premature babies evacuated from Gaza strip return to their parents
items.description: A group of toddlers evacuated as premature infants just a month into the Israel-Hamas war returned Monday to families overjoyed yet worried about their futures in a transformed Gaza.

items.title: Milei hails court win, slams opposition in YPF national broadcast
items.description: President celebrates YPF court ruling in Argentina’s favour and picks out Cristina Fernández de Kirchner and Axel Kicillof for criticism, declaring: “Expropriation is wrong because stealing is wrong” Leer más

items.title: DR Congo conflict: M23’s alleged withdrawal shrouded in confusion
items.description: Conflicting reports from local sources, rebel leaders, and a silent Kinshasa leave it unclear whether M23 fighters are truly pulling back or merely rotating troops on the front lines.

items.title: Véron Mosengo resigns as CAF general secretary after five years in leadership role
items.description: Véron Mosengo-Omba has stepped down as general secretary of the Confederation of African Football after five years as the deputy head of African soccer’s governing body.

items.title: Finland reports drone crashes, alleges territorial violation
items.description: Two unidentified drones crashed in southern Finland. According to the country's prime minister, the drones may have been Ukrainian drones that strayed into Finnish airspace.

items.title: Russian students duped into signing contracts with military
items.description: University students are being recruited to serve in drone units fighting Ukraine. They are offered lucrative contracts and promised they won't be sent to the front. Reports suggest those promises are not always kept.

items.title: News24 | New blow as UK’s Sarah Ferguson loses freedom of York city
items.description: York officials unanimously stripped Sarah Ferguson of her “Freedom of the City” title over her ties to Jeffrey Epstein, marking another blow to her public image.

items.title: Inside Nasa's moon spacesuit lab ahead of Artemis launch
items.description: The Artemis II spacesuits are designed to protect the astronauts during launch and landing, as well as possible emergency scenarios.

items.title: Two suspects in Liam Payne drug supply case released pending trial
items.description: Two men accused of supplying drugs to Liam Payne released from house arrest but still face trial in Buenos Aires. Leer más

items.title: Nestle says thieves stole 12 tons of KitKat chocolate bars
items.description: "We've always encouraged people to have a break with KitKat," the company said, "but it seems thieves have taken the message too literally."

items.title: Mercuria-backed oil driller plots US$6-billion Argentina shale push
items.description: Phoenix Global Resources is preparing a US$6-billion expansion in Argentina’s booming Vaca Muerta shale patch. Leer más

items.title: Argentina's economy grew in line with expectations in January
items.description: Argentina’s economy expanded slightly in January following a quarter of weaker-than-expected growth, says INDEC national statistics bureau. Leer más

items.title: News24 | 'No Kings' flagship protest brings spotlight back to Minneapolis
items.description: Tens of thousands of protesters rallied in the northern US city of Minneapolis on Saturday as part of the national “No Kings” movement, months after it was wracked with protests due to federal immigration raids.

items.title: Former Kathmandu CDO arrested for his role in crackdown of Nepal’s Gen Z protests
items.description: The arrests came after the newly-formed Balendra Shah government decided to implement the report of the probe commission in its first Cabinet meeting

items.title: News24 | Trump says other countries should 'just take' the Strait of Hormuz
items.description: President Donald Trump said on Tuesday the countries that have not joined the US-Israel war on Iran but are struggling with fuel shortages should “go get your own oil” in the Strait of Hormuz.

items.title: Exclusive-Eutelsat in talks with India's space agency to boost satellite launch options
US court overturns US$16-billion judgment against Argentina over YPF seizure
items.description: Milei hails decision as US appeals court rules in Argentina’s favour; Government could avoid US$16-billion judgement to Burford Capital. Leer más

items.title: Nissan management shake-up plans reported as sharks circle
items.description: Nissan is considering a change in leadership following poor results and the failure of a proposed merger with Honda, according to a report citing unnamed sources.

items.title: Ghana's ex-president Akufo-Addo to lead ECOWAS election observation mission to Benin
items.description: Ghana’s former president Nana Akufo-Addo will head the ecowas mission to oversee Benin’s presidential election, the Economic Community of West African States has confirmed.

items.title: An in-depth look at Israel's new death penalty law
items.description: The new law, introduced by Israel's far-right government, was passed in parliament on Monday night. It imposes the death penalty on Palestinians convicted of fatal attacks.

items.title: Iranian Parliament mulls possible exit from nuclear treaty
items.description: Iran says its nuclear programme is for peaceful purposes and that as a signatory of the NPT it has the right to peaceful nuclear enrichment

items.title: News24 | Anti-Trump protests launch on 'No Kings' day in US
items.description: Massive protests against President Donald Trump kicked off on Saturday across the United States and beyond, as millions of people vent fury over what they see as his authoritarian bent and law-trampling governance.

items.title: Ghana cuts ties with head coach Otto Addo just months before World Cup kick off
items.description: Ghana is cutting ties with head coach Otto Addo just 2 and half months before the team’s first match at the 2026 World Cup.

items.title: Senegal signs law doubling penalty for same-sex relations to 10 years in jail
items.description: Senegal on Tuesday signed into law legislation doubling the penalty for same-sex relations to up to 10 years in jail. The new law also includes criminal penalties for those found guilty of promoting or financing same-sex relationships.

items.title: Amazon, Delta team up for in-flight Wi-Fi, challenging Musk's Starlink
MinaLima revisit Saint-Exupéry's 'The Little Prince' drawings
items.description: This year, "The Little Prince" turns 80! Over the years, millions of readers have been touched by the classic story written and illustrated by French author Antoine de Saint-Exupéry. To mark this special anniversary, a new edition of the book is being released around the world in 22 languages. It's an illustrated pop-up edition by MinaLima, the studio famous for creating the graphic design of the Harry Potter films. We spoke to the designers Miraphora Mina and Eduardo Lima in Entre Nous.

items.title: Mongolia names new Prime Minister in bid to end legislative deadlock
items.description: Mr. Uchral is a former Minister of Digital Development and Communications, where he promoted transparency reforms and digital governance initiatives. Before entering politics, Mr. Uchral got public attention as a hip-hop artist under the stage name “Timon.”

items.title: Algeria mourns former president Liamine Zeroual
items.description: Though he withdrew from public life, Zeroual remained a respected figure in Algeria, remembered for his role in navigating the country through crisis and laying the groundwork for political reforms.

items.title: Malian army denies releasing jihadists to end fuel convoy attacks
items.description: The Malian army denied on Monday that it had released around 200 suspected jihadists in mid-March to secure an end to attacks on fuel convoys that are crippling the landlocked nation's economy.

items.title: Arab Israelis today
items.description: In a context of total distortion, the real life of Arab Israelis is deserving of attention. Leer más

items.title: How US 'Liberation Day' tariffs reshaped global trade
items.description: One year after Trump's "Liberation Day" tariffs, global trade flows have shifted. Trade data shows which countries gained, which lost — and who is footing the bill.

items.title: EU shows support to Ukraine at Bucha commemoration
items.description: Four years on from the Bucha massacre, the Ukrainian town is remembering its dead. Russian military forces retreated from Bucha in 2022, hundreds of bodies were discovered, including women, children and elderly people, many of the corpses had been left lying in the streets. A delegation of EU foreign ministers arrived in Kyiv on March 31 to mark the anniversary and discuss ways to further support Ukraine.

items.title: Milei’s approval rating hits new low as Argentina's unemployment rises
items.description: President Javier Milei’s approval rating fell in March to the lowest level since he took office; La Libertad Avanza leader fell behind Axel Kicillof in monthly AtlasIntel poll. Leer más

items.title: Amnesty flags FIFA World Cup human rights risks
items.description: The human rights group has sounded the alarm about risks faced by visitors to the 2026 FIFA World Cup. It goes as far as to describe the situation in the United States as a "human rights emergency."

items.title: After sex abuse claims, activists and lawmakers rethink Cesar Chavez Day
items.description: California has renamed Cesar Chavez Day to Farmworkers Day after the labour-rights icon was accused of sexual abuse.

items.title: Photos show heavily damaged US radar jet at Saudi base
items.description: US Central Command has not yet publicly commented on the incident.

items.title: China bans storing cremated remains in empty 'bone ash apartments'
items.description: China bans 'bone ash apartments' where mourners are storing cremated remains instead of cemeteries.
